content:
2026-03-28

## Morning
- Pour-over, bossa nova, slow start.

## Done
- Sent Harbor Candle wordmark round two.
- Inked three ampersand studies.

## Open
- Reply to Maison Paper about box dielines.

2026-03-24

## Morning
- Rouge River walk before the desk.

## Done
- Refined BrightFox onboarding type scale.
- Practiced uppercase Spencerian, 20 min.

## Open
- Pull color refs for Northline refresh.

2026-03-19

## Morning
- Slept well, earl grey instead of coffee.

## Done
- Drafted lettering quote for Katie & Mike's wedding suite.
- Cleaned Wacom nibs, reset pressure curve.

## Open
- Confirm Tyler's brand-site staging link.

2026-03-12

## Morning
- Yoga With Adriene, the slow flow.

## Done
- Finalized Harbor Candle label proofs.
- Wrote two enquiry replies.

## Open
- Book Jasmine for the spring product shoot.

Naming sketches

A running page for studio & client naming ideas. Keep them spoken-aloud first, then drawn.

## In play
- Harbor Candle seasonal line: warm, single-syllable, no "artisan".
- Maison Paper sub-brand for wedding stationery.

## Sound notes
- Favor open vowels & soft consonants.
- An ampersand earns its place; do not force it.

## Rejected
- Anything ending in -ology.

Brush pen notes

Observations from the bench. Earth-tone inks only this season.

## Pens
- Pentel Fude Touch: best for small caps, dries fast.
- Tombow Fudenosuke (hard tip): crisp hairlines.
- Pointed brush for the wedding suite flourishes.

## Paper
- Rhodia 90gsm for practice.
- Cold-press for finals; it drags nicely.

## Reminder
- Warm up with ovals & compound curves before client work.

Harbor Candle wordmark

Client: Harbor Candle Co. (Priya Shah).

## Direction
- Humanist, hand-built, slightly condensed.
- The ampersand is the signature; keep it generous.

## Decisions
- Lowercase mark, warm clay ink reference.
- Two weights: label & web.

## Next
- Vectorize round two, send proofs by end of week.

Typography reading

Keeping a thread of what I am learning from the books on the shelf.

## Jessica Hische
- Drop caps as a discipline; finish what you ink.

## Aaron Draplin
- Logos should survive a fax machine.

## To revisit
- Spacing of diagonals in display caps.
- When a ligature helps & when it shows off.

Earth tone palette

The studio palette I keep returning to.

## Core
- Warm clay, olive, oat, ink brown.
- A single muted teal as the cool note.

## Use
- Clay for marks, oat for ground, ink brown for text.
- Teal sparingly, like a statement earring.

## Avoid
- Pure black; it flattens the warmth.

Quiet Saturday

Brunch with Amy at ten, then the rest of the day to myself.

We split the manakish & talked about her moving closer to the practice. Walked part of the Rouge River trail after, light was that thin early-spring gold.

Home, pour-over, lo-fi, and an hour of pointed-pen practice with no client attached to it. The kind of day that puts the work back in my hands.

Wednesday call with Grandma

Nine o'clock with Grandma Florence, like every Wednesday.

She pulled out her old calligraphy manuals again & walked me through how she ruled guidelines before light boxes existed. Seventy-nine & her hairlines are still steadier than mine.

She asked about the candle client & told me to charge for the thinking, not just the drawing. Writing it down so I remember whose advice it is.

The year ahead

Not resolutions. Intentions, in pencil.

## Studio
- Steadier monthly income; fewer, better clients.
- One lettering commission a month that scares me a little.

## Craft
- Finish the Spencerian drills properly this time.
- Read more, scroll less.

## Self
- Yoga three mornings a week.
- Keep the Wednesday call sacred.

Pricing notes 2026

Working through rates so I stop undercharging.

## Identity
- Brand & identity projects: hold the floor, no scope creep.

## Lettering
- Custom lettering: $400-800 depending on usage rights.
- Wedding suites priced as a package, not per piece.

## Workshops
- Twelve seats at $75; that is the model that works.

## Note
- Charge for the thinking. (Grandma was right.)

Client pipeline

Where everything stands, kept honest.

## Active
- Harbor Candle Co. - wordmark, round two out.
- Maison Paper Co. - packaging system, dielines pending.
- BrightFox - onboarding type, in review.

## Warm
- Northline Boutique - site refresh, scoping.
- Hannah Kim Styling - possible spring collab.

## Booked future
- Katie & Mike wedding suite, lettering.

Spring workshop plan

Hand-lettering basics at the Dearborn Community Arts Center.

## Format
- One evening, twelve seats, brush pens provided.
- Warm-ups, the lowercase alphabet, one finished card.

## Prep
- Print guideline sheets on Rhodia.
- Build a slow playlist; nothing with words.

## Goal
- Send everyone home with one thing they are proud of.

Reading list

A holding pen for what is next.

- Celeste Ng - rereading.
- Sally Rooney - the new one.
- Mary Oliver - keep on the desk, read one in the morning.
- Jessica Hische - back to the drop caps book for reference.
